prose:
Method–provenance summary (to reproduce):

• Property: atom‐condensed Fukui functions f⁺ (electrophilic), f⁻ (nucleophilic), f⁰ (=½(f⁺+f⁻)), and dual descriptor, plus identification of the most electrophilic/nucleophilic sites for acrolein (C₃H₄O).
• Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’ = B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting; exact four-center integrals) in PySCF 2.x via chemkit.
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied fixed Cartesian coordinates (file  /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/acrolein/acrolein.xyz ); no further optimisation — single-point N, N ± 1 calculations (`DFT//input xyz`).
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
• Electronic state: charge 0, multiplicity 1 (singlet).  Cation state: +1, doublet; anion state: –1, doublet.
• Convergence & integrity: SCF cycles for neutral, cation, and anion all converged; integrity.status = “ok”, trustworthy = true.
• Live log: calculation ran with chemkit skill `fukui-reactivity`; engine stdout was streamed to the live log file announced at launch (path recorded in the session’s working directory).
• Command (from result JSON, abridged):
  ```
  …/cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 …
  ```
